title: Bonyad Mostazafan founded from confiscated Pahlavi assets
description: Ayatollah Khomeini decrees the creation of the Bonyad-e Mostazafan (Foundation of the Oppressed), successor to the Pahlavi Foundation, to administer assets confiscated from the former royal family and dozens of associates of the old regime; within a decade its holdings reportedly exceed $20bn across

title: Nationalization of banks and major industries
description: Revolutionary Council nationalizes the banking system and large private industrial/commercial conglomerates, restructuring much of the economy under state and bonyad control.

title: US freezes Iranian assets
description: Executive Order 12170 freezes roughly $12 billion in Iranian assets in the US following the embassy hostage crisis (Nov 4, 1979 – Jan 20, 1981).

title: US trade embargo
description: United States severs diplomatic relations and imposes a full trade embargo on Iran.

title: Iran-Iraq War begins
description: Eight-year war (1980-1988) imposes massive fiscal costs, disrupts oil exports, and entrenches a rationing/coupon system for basic goods.

title: Wartime rationing coupon system introduced
description: Amid the Iraqi invasion, a trade squeeze and food shortages, the government begins distributing ration coupons for sugar, rice, meat, cooking oil and other staples through neighborhood cooperatives; the coupon ('koopon') system remains in force through the 1980s and is phased out only toward the end

title: Algiers Accords partially unfreeze Iranian assets
description: The Algiers Accords end the 444-day hostage crisis: the US agrees to unblock roughly $7.9-8bn of the approximately $12bn in Iranian assets frozen in November 1979, transfers 50 tonnes of gold held at the New York Federal Reserve, and creates the Iran-US Claims Tribunal at The Hague, with Iran deposi

title: Usury-free Islamic banking law enacted
description: Parliament passes the Law for Usury (Interest)-Free Banking, taking effect 20 March 1984, restructuring the entire banking system around profit-and-loss-sharing contracts (mosharekat, mozarebeh) rather than fixed interest, with Bank Markazi thereafter setting minimum/maximum expected profit-share ra

title: US bans imports of Iranian-origin goods
description: Executive Order 12613 prohibits importing any goods or services of Iranian origin into the United States, citing Iranian state support for terrorism and attacks on US-flag and other neutral shipping in the Persian Gulf during the Iran-Iraq War; exceptions cover news materials, petroleum products ref

title: Iran-Iraq War ends — ceasefire takes effect
description: Iran notifies the UN Secretary-General on 17-18 July 1988 that it accepts Security Council Resolution 598, after a deteriorating war economy and a string of Iraqi battlefield gains; the ceasefire takes effect at 3am on 20 August 1988 under UNIIMOG monitoring, ending eight years of war and opening th

title: First Post-War Five-Year Plan (Rafsanjani reconstruction)
description: Rafsanjani government begins post-war economic liberalization and reconstruction planning after Khomeini's death (June 1989).

title: Manjil-Rudbar earthquake
description: M7.3 earthquake strikes Gilan and Zanjan provinces -- Iran's deadliest and costliest natural disaster of the modern era: an estimated 40,000 people killed, 105,000 injured, and roughly 100,000 houses destroyed. NOAA's Significant Earthquake Database estimates $7.2 billion in contemporaneous damage,

title: External debt crisis forces payment moratorium
description: After the postwar reconstruction boom's heavy short-term borrowing (foreign debt reaching roughly $28bn) collides with weaker oil prices, the Central Bank effectively halts most external debt-service payments and new borrowing dries up; imports are cut nearly in half in 1994 and external debt peaks

title: Exchange-rate unification attempt
description: Rafsanjani government attempts to unify Iran's multiple-tier exchange rate system; the effort partially unwinds after reserve pressure.

title: US bans Iranian petroleum-development deals
description: Executive Order 12957 declares a national emergency with respect to Iran and prohibits US persons from financing, managing or supervising the development of Iranian petroleum resources, a narrower precursor to the comprehensive US trade and investment ban imposed two months later (Executive Order 12

title: US comprehensive trade and investment ban
description: Executive Order 12959 bars virtually all US trade and investment with Iran.

title: Iran-Libya Sanctions Act (ILSA)
description: US law imposes secondary sanctions on foreign firms investing over $40 million/year (later $20M) in Iran's energy sector.

title: Official/market exchange-rate unification
description: Iran unifies its official and market exchange rates at the start of the 1381 fiscal year, a rare successful unification episode.

title: Bam earthquake
description: M6.6 earthquake destroys much of the historic city of Bam in Kerman province, killing an estimated 31,000 people and leveling the UNESCO-listed Arg-e Bam citadel; NOAA's Significant Earthquake Database estimates $500 million in direct damage, while a subsequent World Bank post-disaster assessment pu

title: Article 44 privatization policy issued
description: Supreme Leader Khamenei issues the 'General Policies of Article 44,' ordering transfer of up to 80% of shares in large state-owned enterprises (excluding core sectors such as upstream oil) to the private and cooperative sectors via public share offerings, reviving a stalled privatization program.

title: UNSC Resolution 1696 demands enrichment suspension
description: The Security Council adopts Resolution 1696 (14-1, Qatar opposed), invoking Chapter VII Article 40 to demand Iran suspend all uranium-enrichment-related and reprocessing activities by 31 August 2006 or face the prospect of economic and diplomatic sanctions under Article 41; Iran does not comply, and

title: First Justice Shares distributed
description: Under the Article 44 privatization framework, the government begins distributing subsidized 'Justice Shares' (Saham-e Edalat) in state-owned enterprises to low-income households, war veterans' families and Basij members; by December 2006 roughly 4.6 million Iranians have received shares worth about

title: First UN Security Council sanctions
description: UNSC Resolution 1737 imposes the first multilateral sanctions over Iran's nuclear program, targeting proliferation-sensitive trade and assets.

title: Mehr Housing Scheme launched
description: President Ahmadinejad launches the Maskan-e Mehr affordable-housing program, aiming to build millions of subsidized units on state land for low-income families; the Central Bank is directed to print money to fund the scheme, expanding the monetary base and contributing to a roughly nine-fold rise in

title: UNSC Resolution 1747 expands arms embargo
description: Resolution 1747 bans Iran from exporting any arms or related materiel, calls on states and international financial institutions not to enter new grants, financial assistance or concessional loans with the Iranian government except for humanitarian or developmental purposes, and freezes the assets of
